description: Microsoft Corporation develops, licenses, and supports software, services, and devices. Segments include Productivity & Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud (Azure), and Personal Computing.
known_facts: Azure is Microsoft's cloud platform and the second-largest cloud provider
Microsoft Office 365 and Microsoft 365 generate significant subscription revenue
Microsoft acquired Activision Blizzard for $69 billion in 2023
GitHub was acquired by Microsoft in 2018
Microsoft has a strategic partnership and investment in OpenAI
Windows remains a major product line for PC operating systems
known_risks: Antitrust scrutiny over Activision acquisition and cloud market practices
Competition from Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud in cloud computing
AI integration risks and costs from OpenAI partnership
Regulatory pressure in Europe regarding cloud and software bundling
known_strengths: Azure growing at double-digit rates with enterprise sticky workloads
Diversified revenue across cloud, software, gaming, and LinkedIn
Strong enterprise relationships and Microsoft 365 adoption
Copilot AI integration across product suite
Massive free cash flow generation enabling R&D and acquisitions
Strong developer ecosystem through GitHub and Azure DevOps
